In this video, we'll take you on a journey through the city's top 16 attractions and experiences, showcasing the diverse range of activities that make Austin a must-visit destination. videos.transcript: [Music] hey everyone today we're exploring some of my favorite spots in the vibrant and eclectic Austin Texas whether you're looking to hike to a waterfall explore world-class museums or Cruise the lake in search of bats Austin has something for everyone I've been here many times over the years and I'm still finding new places every time I go I originally included food on this list as well but I have so many favorites that I had to break that out into another video so let's jump into my top recommendations for the City of Austin and be sure to let me know what I left off in the comments my first recommendation is to see the bats if you visit Austin anytime from March to October you can't leave the city without seeing the bats living under the Congress Avenue Bridge Austin is home to the largest urban bat colony in the world with over 1.5 million Mexican free-tailed bats residing there the bats leave the bridge nightly right around Sunset which is a sight to see they fly around in a circle and once enough bats gather they all head out from the bridge along the water get there about 30 to 45 minutes early to get a good spot to watch them from also note that they only come out if it's warm enough and it's not raining another great way to see them is on the sightseeing Cruise which is our next recommendation if you're looking to do a sightseeing cruise I recommend doing the cruise that will take you to see the bats from the water at the end but if you visit when the bats aren't there it's still worth taking a cruise along Lady Bird Johnson Lake at sunset these cruises are well priced and take you down the water slowly along the way the guide will tell you the history of the bridges the lake and downtown Austin we're going to learn about the city and to experience the beauty of ladybird Lake plus if you take the bat Cruise you get to watch the bats fly out from under the bridge while you sit at a great vantage point on the water next up we have the LBJ Presidential Library and Museum an interesting spot to learn more about the 36th president a famous Texas resident his Ranch is located in the Texas Hill Country about an hour outside the city if you want to see that as well the library and Museum are situated on the University of Texas campus and have a great collection of photographs and documents from the president's time in office it also has a replica Oval Office on the top floor and Lady Bird Johnson's office which looks out over the city right next to it the most impressive part was the large archives area which you can see from the main hall it's imposing and not something you often see at the more modern presidential libraries following that we have the Bullock State History Museum this museum is a must visit spot in Austin if you're a fan of Texas history this museum is large and interactive with many great exhibits on everything from the Alamo and Texas ranching to Austin City Limits you can spend a few hours here walking the different floors and watching the educational videos they have on many of the vital pieces of Texas history there's even an entire ship sitting on the Museum's first floor which gives you a little understanding of the Museum's size don't forget to take a photo with a 35 foot tall bronze star outside the building when you're done following that we have Peter Pan mini golf this mini golf spot has been here since 1948 and has two different courses with varying difficulty levels Peter Pan mini golf is known for the sculptures that stand on many of the holes with everything from a clown head to a T-Rex while it's not difficult it's a fun way to spend an hour in the city next up we have McKinney Falls State Park this is a beautiful Natural Area only 15 minutes outside of the city with many miles of hiking trails the park has an upper and lower Falls area with the water cascading over limestone rocks into the pools below these waterfalls are only 10 to 15 feet tall but they are still beautiful to see when they're flowing plus when the water level is low you can cross over and see an Old Homestead that has been preserved in the park it's a great way to get out in nature while in Austin [Music] following that we have Ladybird Johnson Wildflower Center alternatively if you're visiting in the spring you should consider going to The Wildflower Center named after the famous first lady a well-known advocate for nature preservation this area is one of the most beautiful botanical gardens I've ever seen it seamlessly integrates with the nature surrounding it and has miles of hiking trails through many different fields of flowers be sure to climb the tower in the middle to view the entire center from above and visit in the spring when the Blue Bonnets bloom next up we have Barton Springs situated in the beautiful Zilker Park and fed by underground Springs Barton Springs has cool and clear water that forms a unique public pool the small concrete Dam that created the pool was built in 1918. I haven't gotten a chance to swim there has always been too cold when I visited but the pool is historic and a fun summer meeting place for Austin plus the water is around 68 degrees year round next up we have Mount Bonnell offering breathtaking panoramic views of the Austin Skyline Colorado River and surrounding Hill Country a visit to mount Bunnell is a must for nature lovers and photography enthusiasts the walk to the top of the 775 foot tall mountain is via a series of stone steps it can be a little steep on the way up but once you make it to the top you can watch the Ridgeline and take in many Fantastic views of the surrounding area it's an ideal spot for a leisurely hike a romantic sunset or an invigorating morning workout next up we have the penny backer Bridge known for its distinct Arch design and Scenic setting the penny backer bridge is an iconic Austin landmark while it's fun to drive across the best views are from hiking the trail on the north side of the bridge there are about a dozen spots here for cars that run along the side of the road and the hike up is not official so it can be Steep and slippery the views from the top of the cliffs below the bridge and the Colorado River are amazing note that I saw a good amount of Glass on the ground when I was there so I wouldn't recommend leaving anything of value in your car during the short hike next up we have Zilker Botanical Garden a visit to Zilker Botanical Garden offers an enchanting experience for all ages with its diverse collection of plants themed Gardens and picturesque Landscapes the garden is a Haven for nature lovers and photographers or those just looking for a peaceful retreat in the city the park has over a mile of trails and I loved the way that they integrated art throughout the garden it's a beautiful place to explore for the whole family and one of the most relaxing places I've been to in Austin next up I recommend walking along Lady Bird Lake while we talked about getting on the water at ladybird Lake earlier you should also walk bike or simply experience the lake from the shore the path that runs along the South Side provides stunning views of the city skyline and has some fun attractions like the Stevie Ray Vaughan statue I also love the portion with the elevated walkway that goes out over the water and have seen everything from Turtles to Abundant bird life while strolling the lake it's a fantastic place to unwind exercise or enjoy a walk next up we have all the murals around Austin the vibrant ever-evolving street art scene in Austin adds a unique charm to the city the murals are situated all over the city and you can find maps online that will take you to some of the most popular my favorite are the Austin mural near 6th Street and the pizza astronaut which may only be there for a short time as the business it was situated behind is closed exploring the downtown murals offers an opportunity to appreciate the local talent and add a colorful twist to your Austin visit next up for quirky and offbeat experience visit the Museum of weird this small museum has an array of oddities Curiosities and unusual artifacts that are sure to pique your interest the museum can be seen in less than a half hour but it's situated on three floors with many different pieces to interact with and enjoy I love things like this so it was a highlight for me in Austin and I'm sure it would be a memorable and fun spot for those who appreciate the unconventional next up we have the Texas state capitol the Texas state capitol is the government home for the state and an architectural Marvel that offers free guided tours allowing visitors to learn more about the Lone Star State's Rich past the building was built in 1888 and has been updated a few times over the years it's stunning to explore especially the large rotunda in the middle of the lobby you can climb multiple floors and see it from a few different angles also the beautiful Capital grounds are perfect for a relaxing walk in the city next up we have South Congress Avenue also known as Soco no trip to Austin is complete without a visit to this area Soco is a vibrant and bustling cultural Hub that showcases Austin's creative Spirit here you can explore Austin's funky and eclectic Side by strolling down the unique shops restaurants food trucks and live music venues a few of my favorite places there are Joe's Coffee Home Slice Pizza and the Continental Club there are some great murals to see in this part of Austin as well following that we have the Blanton Museum of Art this Museum showcases a diverse collection of artwork from local National and international artists the museum is located on the University of Texas campus and it has over 21 000 pieces of art in its permanent collection it's a great place to spend a few hours or longer if you're an art Enthusiast be sure to check out Ellsworth Kelly's Austin building outside of the main Museum this stunning piece is especially beautiful when the light is shining in the colorful windows I was planning to include food but since Austin is such a fantastic Food City I'll cover those recommendations in another video that's it for my top spots in Austin let me know what I left off in the comments and we'll see you on the next video

videos.title: Top 20 Things to Do in Austin Texas!
videos.description:
Austin, Texas is a city full of outdoor beauty, culture, and unique attractions. Some of the best things to do in Austin include walking or biking along Lady Bird Lake, cooling off at Barton Springs Pool, and enjoying a picnic or festival at Zilker Park. For incredible views of the city, head to Mount Bonnell, one of the most iconic Austin attractions.

Visitors can also explore downtown Austin landmarks, discover fascinating exhibits at the Bullock Texas State History Museum, and visit the impressive Texas State Capitol. Nature lovers will appreciate Austin’s many hiking trails, gardens, and scenic spots, perfect for a relaxing getaway.

Whether you’re planning a family vacation or just a weekend getaway, this Austin travel guide covers the top things to do in Austin Texas, from outdoor adventures to cultural experiences and delicious eats.

videos.transcript: Welcome to Austin, Texas, where the vibe is as hot as the weather and the adventures never stop. Whether you're here for the food, the music, or just some good old Texas charm, this city has something for everyone. From watching the famous bats soar over Congress Avenue Bridge to cooling off at natural wonders like McKini Falls and diving into local history at unique museums, Austin blends nature, culture, and weirdness in the best way possible. One of the most iconic things to do in Austin is to witness the nightly bat flight at the Congress Avenue Bridge. Every evening from late March through October, around 1.5 million Mexican free tailed bats emerge from under the bridge just after sunset. It's the largest urban bat colony in North America and a true natural wonder right in the heart of the city. It's an unforgettable site as they swirl into the sky in search of insects, creating a natural spectacle that draws locals and tourists alike. For a more unique and cooler view, hop on the water. You can rent a kayak, paddle board, or retro boat, or even book a sunset bat watching cruise on Ladybird Lake. Seeing the bats emerge from beneath the bridge while you float on the lake is an experience like no other. peaceful, scenic, and just the right amount of weird Austin style. Plan to arrive about 30 to 45 minutes before sunset for the best viewing, and be ready with your camera. No trip to Austin is complete without a visit to the Texas State Capital, the crown jewel of downtown and one of the most impressive capital buildings in the country. Standing taller than the US capital in Washington DC, this historic landmark is made from Texas pink granite and surrounded by beautifully manicured grounds and monuments. You can take a free guided tour or explore on your own to learn about Texas history, politics, and the building's incredible architecture. Don't miss the stunning rotunda, portraits of past governors, and the whispering gallery under the dome. Whether you're into history, architecture, or just looking for a great photo op, the capital is a must-see Austin highlight. If you're looking to dive deep into the story of Texas, the Bulock Texas State History Museum is a must- vvisit spot in Austin. Just north of the capital, this striking building with its bronze lone star and copper dome showcases over 16,000 years of Texas history across three dynamic floors. Inside, you'll find everything from Native American artifacts and a 300-year-old shipwreck to exhibits on the Texas Revolution, space exploration, and modern pop culture. The museum also features the biggest IMAX screen in Texas, plus the immersive Texas Spirit Theater. Visitors of all ages will enjoy interactive displays, bilingual exhibits, and special events. The museum is open daily from 10:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m. and admission is free on the first Sunday of each month. Whether it's your first visit or your 10th, the Bulock Museum offers a powerful look at what makes Texas unique. Located on the University of Texas campus, the Blandon Museum of Art is one of the top cultural attractions in Austin. With a collection of over 21,000 works, it features everything from European classics and contemporary art to standout pieces from Latin America and modern print collections. A major highlight is Ellsworth Kelly's Austin, a one-of-a-kind building that blends art, architecture, and light into a truly immersive experience. Visitors can also enjoy rotating exhibitions, outdoor installations, and special events like live music and free family days. The museum is open Tuesday through Sunday and admission is free on Tuesdays. Whether you're an art lover or just looking for a peaceful, inspiring place to explore, the Bllandon is a mustsee. When the Texas heat kicks in, there's no better place to cool off than Barton Springs Pool. A natural springfed swimming spot located in Zilker Park, just minutes from downtown Austin. This iconic 3 acre pool stays a refreshing 68 to 70 degrees Fahrenheit year round, making it a local favorite for both relaxing and swimming laps. Located in the heart of Austin's Zilker Park, the Zilker Botanical Garden spans 28 acres of beautifully designed landscapes. Visitors can wander through peaceful spaces like the Tanaguchi Japanese Garden, admire real dinosaur tracks in the Hartman Prehistoric Garden, and take in the color and fragrance of the Rose Garden with streams, waterfalls, koi ponds, and shaded walking paths. It's a perfect place to relax or snap some great photos. Open daily, admission ranges from $6 to $8 with free entry on select days and discounts available through community programs. Whether you're into plants, photography, or simply want a peaceful escape, Zilker Botanical Garden is a must- vvisit spot in Austin, just minutes from downtown Austin, McKini Falls State Park offers a peaceful escape into nature. Visitors can swim at the scenic upper and lower falls. Hike nearly 9 m of trails and explore historic ruins from the 1850s. With camping, fishing, and picnicking available, it's a perfect spot for a day trip or weekend getaway in the heart of Texas. Located on the University of Texas campus in Austin, the LBJ Presidential Library honors the life and legacy of President Lyndon B. Johnson. Visitors can explore exhibits on civil rights, the Vietnam War, and the Great Society. Highlights include a replica of LBJ's Oval Office, Ladybird Johnson's recreated office, and even a moon rock from the Apollo missions. With powerful multimedia displays and millions of historic documents, the library offers a fascinating look into one of America's most pivotal decades. Just 30 minutes north of Austin, Inner Space Cavern is a stunning underground cave discovered by highway workers in 1963. Formed over 20 million years ago, the cavern is filled with dramatic stelactites, stelagmites, and even ice age fossils of saber-tooththed cats and mammoths. Visitors can choose from several tours, including an easylit walk or a wild crawl through adventure. With a constant temperature around 72° F, it's a perfect year-round activity for families and explorers alike. Mount Benell offers one of the best panoramic views in Austin, overlooking the Colorado River, Hill Country, and downtown skyline. It's a favorite for sunsets, casual hikes, and photo ops, reached by a short climb of limestone steps. While the overlook itself is peaceful and scenic, visitors should be cautious in the parking area. Car break-ins have been reported, so it's best not to leave valuables inside your vehicle. When we visited, we saw a lot of broken glass in the parking. Tucked away just a short walk from Mount Benell, Mayfield Park and Nature Preserve is one of Austin's most peaceful hidden gems. This serene spot features lush gardens, lilyfilled koi ponds, and colorful peacocks that freely roam the grounds. Surrounding the garden is a 21 acre nature preserve with quiet hiking trails shaded by native trees, offering a perfect escape into nature without leaving the city. Free to visit and open daily, Mayfield Park is a tranquil place to relax, explore, and enjoy a slower pace. Nicknamed the sunset capital of Texas, the oasis is a must- vvisit restaurant perched high above Lake Travis about 30 minutes from downtown Austin. Known for its multi-level decks and sweeping lake views, it's one of the best places in the area to watch the sun dip below the horizon. The food is TexMex inspired with options like fajitas, enchiladas, and margaritas. But the main attraction is the scenery. Whether you're stopping by for dinner or just a drink and a view, the Oasis delivers an unforgettable Austin moment, especially at Golden Hour. Walk through Austin and you'll find the city itself is a gallery. Colorful murals brighten walls across neighborhoods like South Congress, East Austin, and downtown. Each one telling a story of creativity, culture, and local pride. Rent a bike or walk around South Congress and East 11th Street. Each piece offers a perfect backdrop for photos. If you're looking for something unique and quirky in Austin, the Museum of the Weird is a mustsee. Nestled on Sixth Street, this one-of-a-kind museum is packed with bizarre and fascinating exhibits. From mummies and shrunken heads to oddities like Bigfoot, the museum celebrates all things strange and unusual. It's a perfect stop if you love the bizarre, the unexplained, or just want to experience something totally offbeat. Whether you're a local or a visitor, the Museum of the Weird is a fun, quirky spot for a family adventure or an unusual date outing. If you travel with small children, Thinkory is a must visit. This children's museum that's perfect for sparking your little one's creativity and curiosity. It's a place where learning feels like play. From water play stations to light labs and outdoor climbing structures, the museum offers tons of opportunities for exploration and discovery. Thinkery is full of engaging activities that will keep your child entertained for hours while also teaching valuable skills. Whether you're a local or just visiting, it's an essential stop for a fun, educational, and family-friendly day out in Austin. Next on Out List is Museum of Ice Cream. This Instagram worthy destination is a dream come true for anyone who loves ice cream, candy, and vibrant, playful art. The museum features immersive, interactive exhibits from a sprinkle pool you can dive into to candy colored rooms that transport you to a world of sugary delight. The Museum of Ice Cream is an unforgettable, sweet escape that's perfect for all ages. Museum of Illusions is interactive museum is filled with optical illusions, mind tricks, and immersive exhibits that will leave you questioning what's real and what's not. From giant Ames rooms that make you look tiny to dizzying vortex tunnels, each exhibit offers a fun way to explore perception and science. Whether you're solving puzzles, posing for photos in quirky rooms, or simply exploring the mind-bending exhibits, there's something for everyone. One of the best ways to experience Austin's natural beauty is by hiking along the Colorado River, locally known as Ladybird Lake. The most popular option is the Anne and Roy Butler Hike and Bike Trail, a scenic 10-mi loop that winds through downtown, offering stunning views of the city skyline, lush greenery, and peaceful water. Austin Aquarium in North Austin is a compact yet highly interactive wildlife center where visitors can handfeed sharks, rays, birds, and reptiles, and even meet lemurs, sloths, and capiaras, making it part aquarium, aviary, and rainforest allinone. No matter your interests, Austin offers something unforgettable for everyone. Spend your days exploring world-class museums, wandering through vibrant art galleries, and admiring the city's famous street murals. Connect with nature on scenic trails or by kaying Ladybird Lake. And don't miss the chance to watch thousands of bats take flight from under the Congress Avenue Bridge at sunset. With its perfect blend of culture, creativity, and outdoor charm, Austin is a city that always leaves a lasting impression. If you think that we missed some good attraction, let us know in the comments. Thank you for watching. Please like and subscribe to my channel if you want to see more videos like this.

Fun things to do in austin extend to outdoor adventures on Lady Bird Lake, where visitors can kayak, paddleboard, or simply stroll along the scenic trails. The Oasis on Lake Travis, known as the "Sunset Capital of Texas," provides breathtaking views and memorable dining experiences.

scene centered around country blues and rock Its many parks and lakes are popular for hiking biking swimming and boating South of the city Formula 1's Circuit of the Americas Raceway has hosted the United States Grand Prix Before we get into the top 10 things to do in Austin we've included links in the description to various discount codes and links to resources of things to do So make sure you check those out At number 10 is the Texas State Capital The Texas State Capital recognized as a national historic landmark is a remarkable facility and one not to miss when visiting Austin The Red Granite Building with its amazing dome which you can visit by climbing four flights of inlaid marble stairs also has a great gift shop and cafeteria to stop by With 17 surrounding monuments to admire together with wonderfully landscaped gardens and a free guided tour to take advantage of the family can hear all about the history and stories of Texas from friendly well-informed guides Take a splash with Austin Duck Adventures at number nine One of Austin's top tours Austin Duck Adventures offers an amphibious tour that includes tours of downtown Austin and beautiful Lake Austin in hydra vehicles On board knowledgeable guides entertain sightseers with facts and stories about local sites and landmarks After their land trek the vehicles pack in their wheels and launch off the shore of Lake Austin for an aquatic tour of the lush scenic lakeside At number eight is Ladybird Lake Hike and Bike Trail Ladybird Lake is a wonderful body of water in the center of this busy city Popular with runners bikers and those just out for a leisurely stroll It's worth taking the family out for an afternoon here Stroll around the waterfront with its many bridges that cross the calm waters and even rent a boat if you're so inclined Paddle boarding kayaking and canoeing are also popular activities around Ladybird Lake At number seven is the Austin Steam Train A fabulous venue for all the train lovers in the family and especially the kids The Austin Steam Train gives the opportunity for everyone to ride on a real vintage passenger train The depot itself is located in Cedar Park and operates on the weekends throughout the year It is staffed by volunteers with refreshments available during the train trips that are served from the concession car At number six is Zilker Botanical Gardens Zilker Botanical Garden is located on 26 acres of fenced land in Austin Texas and has often been called the jewel in the heart of Austin Themed gardens such as rose herb and Japanese gardens are interconnected with streams waterfalls and koi filled ponds The Hartman Prehistoric Garden recreates a local dinosaur habitat The gardens are open to the public most days year round and are enjoyed by thousands of visitors each year Number five is the Westcave Outdoor Discovery Center West Cave is located in Southwest Travis County approximately 45 minutes from downtown Austin The center was built with a specific mission to inspire people to develop a lifelong practice of protecting nature A short hike will take you along a forest trail down some steps to a creek bed with a lovely waterfall surprisingly big cave area and a marvelous collection of old cypress trees and ferns Though the hikes are not strenuous it's best to wear appropriate shoes as the walk down can be a little steep but display in the exhibits including an impressively renovated shipwreck uncovering the past has never been so easy Whether you're intrigued by Tales of the Bush family space exploration or Trails of the Buffalo Soldiers this is one of the best museums in Austin Plus some of the exhibits are interactive meaning it's fun for kids too Number three is the Congress Avenue Bridge Bats From March to October 1.5 million bats emerge nightly from the narrow crevices in the underside of the Anne W Richard's Congress Avenue Bridge They usually start to emerge from the bridge around 20 minutes before sundown The walkway on the east side of the Congress Avenue Bridge offers the best vantage point for watching the bats emerge and fly eastward over Ladybird Lake The hillside below the bridge is a little more kid-friendly since you can spread out a blanket and even have a picnic while you wait At number two is the Austin Nature and Science Center Centrally located on the western edge of Zilker Park and established in 1960 the Austin Nature and Science Center's mission is to provide hands-on educational exhibits The ANSC offers a range of nature-based programs exhibits workshops and a fantastic activity where you can dig for dinosaur fossils There are a variety of temporary and permanent exhibits the naturalist workshop bird watching accessible trails around the pond system and more At number one is the Austin Zoo Located on the southwest edge of Austin Texas Austin Zoo draws visitors from around the world to learn about rescued animals and spend quality time outdoors in the Texas Hill Country The mission of Austin Zoo is to assist animals in need through rescue rehabilitation and education African lions and Bengal tigers stretch out in the big cat habitats while monkeys and marmicet swing from tree to tree in the monkey areas In addition to wild creatures the zoo houses domesticated animals such as miniature donkeys pot-bellied pigs and llamas So that sums up our top 10
